{"author": null, "closed_at": null, "collaborating_teams": null, "collaborating_users": null, "created_at": null, "credits": [], "credits_detailed": [], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-45286", "cvss": {"score": 4.3, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:N/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 4.3, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:N/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}}, "cwe_ids": ["CWE-200"], "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-200", "name": "Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor"}], "description": "### Impact
An authenticated user can enumerate users on the same Nextcloud instance by using the Calendar app's endpoint for suggesting attendees. The sharing restrictions, applied to other endpoints, were not effective here.

### Patches
It is recommended that the Calendar app is upgraded to 6.2.3 or 5.5.17

### Workarounds
* Disable Calendar app

### References
* [HackerOne](https://hackerone.com/reports/3540663)
* [PullRequest](https://github.com/nextcloud/calendar/pull/8197)
* [Public ticket](https://github.com/nextcloud/calendar/issues/7971)

### For more information
If you have any questions or comments about this advisory:
* Create a post in [nextcloud/security-advisories](https://github.com/nextcloud/security-advisories/discussions)
* Customers: Open a support ticket at [portal.nextcloud.com](https://portal.nextcloud.com)

title: Calendar app leaked user identifiers via attendee suggestion endpoint
